etymology.description: 舌 (mouth with a forked tongue) with added stroke at top to suggest tongue movement -> speech
etymology.interpretation: The character 言(yán, "speech") depicts the addition of a horizontal stroke to the ancient form of 舌(shé, "tongue") to indicate the motion of the tongue as it moves to articulate speech. This directly connects the organ involved in speaking to the concept of speech itself.

examples.chinese: 他的言语充满智慧(他的言語充滿智慧)。
examples.english: His speech is full of wisdom.

examples.chinese: 我们应该以行动证明我们的言词(我們應該以行動證明我們的言詞)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi yǐ xíngdòng zhèngmíng wǒmen de yáncí.
examples.english: We should prove our words with actions.

examples.chinese: 他说话的方式显示了他的修养(他說話的方式顯示了他的修養)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shuōhuà de fāngshì xiǎnshìle tā de xiūyǎng.
examples.english: The way he speaks shows his refinement.
